meta.title: Hospice Coverage for Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Members

sections.heading: II.A Hospice Coverage and Member Election

claims.claim: CalOptima Health shall ensure Managed Care Plan Members receive Hospice Care upon election, in accordance with DHCS contract requirements and state law.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks shall be responsible for ensuring the provision of Hospice Care services for Terminally Ill Members

claims.claim: Managed Care Plan Members who elect hospice care remain enrolled in the MCP and continue to receive all non-terminal-illness benefits through the plan.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks, in accordance with the Health Network-specific DOFR, are responsible for the provision of all Medically Necessary Covered Services not related to a Member’s Terminal Illness

claims.claim: Hospice coverage for Managed Care Plan Members is provided in two 90-day benefit periods, beginning on the date of hospice election.
claims.source_text: A Member may elect to receive hospice benefits for two (2) election periods consisting of ninety (90) calendar days each

claims.claim: After the initial two 90-day benefit periods, Managed Care Plan Members are entitled to unlimited 60-day hospice benefit periods.
claims.source_text: an unlimited number of subsequent periods of sixty (60) calendar days each

claims.claim: Each hospice benefit period starts the day the Managed Care Plan Member first receives hospice care and ends when the corresponding 90-day or 60-day period elapses.
claims.source_text: At any time during an election period, a Member may elect to revoke or modify Hospice Care.

claims.claim: CalOptima Health shall ensure Managed Care Plan Members are referred to hospice services in a timely manner, with a target of within 24 hours of request from in-Network hospice Providers.
claims.source_text: Medi-Cal primary Members may access Hospice Care within twenty-four (24) hours of Notification of Hospice request.

claims.claim: Managed Care Plans may restrict hospice coverage to in-Network Providers, unless Medically Necessary services are not available in-Network under Medi-Cal benefits.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health or its Health Networks, in accordance with the Health Network-specific DOFR, shall be responsible for providing Hospice Care to a Medi-Cal primary Member

claims.claim: For out-of-Network hospice Providers, the Managed Care Plan shall seek an agreement — such as a Single Case Agreement or Letter of Agreement — to ensure coverage for the Managed Care Plan Member.
claims.source_text: A Hospice Provider shall be responsible for services related to the Member’s Terminal Illness, including, but not limited to, pharmacy, physician, social services, nursing, home health aide services

claims.claim: Agreements between Managed Care Plans and out-of-Network hospice Providers require the Provider to follow the MCP's documentation and Medical Necessity coverage policies.
claims.source_text: If a hospice provider does not provide adequate clinical documentation or documents do not substantiate the need for General Inpatient Care, the CalOptima Health Medical Director may reduce the Level of Care to Routine Home Care.

sections.heading: V.A Prior Authorization and Utilization Review

claims.claim: Prior Authorization is required by the Managed Care Plan for all hospice services regardless of level of care or benefit period.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health shall not require prior authorization for Hospice Care services under Routine Home Care, Continuous Home Care, and Respite Care levels of Hospice Care.

claims.claim: Managed Care Plans shall review hospice documentation to detect and prevent Fraud, Waste, and Abuse.
claims.source_text: A Hospice Provider shall submit Notification of Hospice election immediately and no later than thirty (30) calendar days after start of services.

meta.title: Utensil Cleaning Guidelines for Nutritional Services

claims.claim: CalOptima Health PACE Centers must serve meals in a clean and safe environment at all times.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health PACE Centers shall serve meals in a clean and safe environment at all times.

claims.claim: CalOptima Health PACE Centers must ensure all utensils used to prepare and serve food to Participants are thoroughly disinfected after each use.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health PACE Centers shall ensure all utensils that are used to prepare and serve food to Participants are thoroughly disinfected after each use.

sections.heading: III.A. CalOptima Health PACE Center utensil cleaning guidelines

claims.claim: Multi-use utensils used for eating, drinking, preparing, and serving of food and drink must be cleaned after each use.
claims.source_text: Multi-use utensils used for eating, drinking, preparing and serving of food and drink shall be cleaned after each use.

claims.claim: Food particles must be removed from utensils by scraping and pre-rinsing in running water.
claims.source_text: Food particles shall be removed by scraping and pre-rinsing in running water.

claims.claim: Multi-use utensils not washed by mechanical means must be thoroughly washed in hot water with a minimum temperature of 43ºC/110ºF using soap or detergent.
claims.source_text: Multi-use utensils not washed by mechanical means shall be thoroughly washed in hot water with a minimum temperature of 43ºC/110ºF using soap, or detergent.

claims.claim: Multi-use utensils not washed by mechanical means must be disinfected by immersion in water containing a bactericidal chemical approved by the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS).
claims.source_text: They shall be rinsed in hot water and disinfected by one (1) of the following methods, or equivalent: a. Immersion in water containing bactericidal chemical approved by the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS).

claims.claim: Multi-use utensils not washed by mechanical means may be disinfected by immersion for at least two minutes in clean water at 77ºC/170ºF.
claims.source_text: Immersion for at least two (2) minutes in clean water at 77ºC/170ºF.

claims.claim: Multi-use utensils not washed by mechanical means may be disinfected by immersion for at least thirty seconds in clean water at 82ºC/180ºF.
claims.source_text: Immersion for at least thirty (30) seconds in clean water at 82ºC/180ºF.

claims.claim: After disinfection, utensils must be allowed to drain and dry in racks or baskets on nonabsorbent surfaces.
claims.source_text: After disinfection, the utensils shall be allowed to drain and dry in racks, or baskets, on nonabsorbent surfaces.

claims.claim: Drying cloths must not be used on utensils after disinfection.
claims.source_text: Drying cloths shall not be used.

claims.claim: Results obtained with dishwashing machines must be equal to those obtained by the manual cleaning methods specified in the CalOptima Health PACE guidelines.
claims.source_text: Results obtained with dishwashing machines shall be equal to those obtained by the methods outlined above

claims.claim: All dishwashing machines must meet the requirements contained in the National Sanitation Foundation, American National Standards Institute, No.3.
claims.source_text: all dishwashing machines shall meet the requirements contained in the National Sanitation Foundation, American National Standards Institute, No.3.

claims.claim: The temperature of the wash solution in spray-type ware washers that use chemicals to sanitize may not be less than 49ºC/120ºF.
claims.source_text: The temperature of the wash solution in the spray-type ware washers that use chemicals to sanitize may not be less than 49ºC/120ºF.

entity_refs: CalOptima Health PACE Quality Improvement Department

claims.claim: The CalOptima Health PACE kitchen employee must record the rinse temperatures in a daily log.
claims.source_text: The CalOptima Health PACE kitchen employee shall record the rinse temperatures in a daily log.

claims.claim: The CalOptima Health PACE Dietician or designee must conduct periodic food safety and sanitation audits to ensure compliance.
claims.source_text: The CalOptima Health PACE Dietician or designee, shall conduct periodic food safety and sanitation audits to ensure compliance

claims.claim: The CalOptima Health PACE Dietician or designee must report any food safety audit issues for follow-up action to the CalOptima Health PACE Quality Improvement Department.
claims.source_text: report any issues for follow-up action to the CalOptima Health PACE Quality Improvement Department.

entity_refs: CalOptima Health PACE Quality Improvement Department

meta.title: Palliative Care Services
entity_refs: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

claims.claim: CalOptima Health provides Palliative Care services to Members in accordance with Senate Bill (SB) 1004, DHCS contractual requirements, and CMS guidance.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health shall provide Palliative Care services to Members as outlined in this Policy, Senate Bill (SB) 1004 and Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) contractual requirements and guidance and the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS).

claims.claim: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks must contract with sufficient Providers that have licensed clinical staff with experience or training in Palliative Care.
claims.source_text: CalOptima Health and its Health Networks shall contract with sufficient Providers that have licensed clinical staff with experience and/or training in Palliative Care in order to provide appropriate access to Palliative Care services.

claims.claim: A CalOptima Health Member who meets the minimum Palliative Care eligibility criteria may continue to access both Palliative Care and Curative Care until the condition improves, stabilizes, or results in death.
claims.source_text: If a Member continues to meet the minimum eligibility criteria described in Section III.A. of this Policy or the pediatric Palliative Care eligibility criteria described in Section III.B. of this Policy, he or she may continue to access both Palliative Care and Curative Care until the condition improves, stabilizes, or results in death.

claims.claim: Palliative Care services under CalOptima Health include Advance Care Planning, including documented discussions addressing Advance Directives such as Physician Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment (POLST).
claims.source_text: Advanced Care Planning, including documented discussions between a physician or other qualified healthcare professional and a Member, family member, or legally recognized decision-maker. Counseling that occurs during these discussions shall address, but not be limited to, Advance Directives, such as Physician Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment (POLST).
